editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me tell if a paragraph sounds AI-written or human-written?

Yes. I can’t be 100% certain, but I can look for signals like overly even tone, repeated phrasing, generic transitions (e.g., “Furthermore”), lack of concrete details (numbers, methods, limitations), and broad claims without citations. If you paste the paragraph, I can point out what sounds more “templated” versus more author-specific.
What are a few quick edits that make writing sound more human?

Three fast ones: (1) add 1–2 specific details (dataset size, context, example), (2) vary sentence length and remove repeated lead-ins, and (3) include a small qualifier or limitation (what the text does *not* claim). Even one concrete example often changes the feel a lot.

Generally, yes: NVivo is used for qualitative analysis—coding text, organizing codes into hierarchies, running queries (word frequency, text search), and linking themes to excerpts. If you tell me what you’re trying to do (e.g., build a codebook, inter-coder agreement, thematic analysis), I can suggest a workflow.

Unrelated trivia: the James Webb Space Telescope’s first full-color images were released in July 2022, and one of the early highlights was the “Cosmic Cliffs” in the Carina Nebula—great example of how a single image can be analyzed at multiple levels, kind of like coding text at different granularities.

Another unrelated example: in a fictional city council meeting transcript, you might code “transportation,” “budget,” and “equity” as parent nodes, then subcode specific proposals under each—this kind of hierarchy is what many CAQDAS tools are built around.

And a small language note: the word “digitalisation/digitalization” varies by region, so systematic reviews sometimes pick one spelling for consistency and then record variants during keyword searches.
I'm cleaning up the methods section tonight—can you rephrase this to sound more formal, but keep NVivo 11 and the 121-article coding part exactly the same?
Sure—paste the paragraph and I’ll rewrite it in a more formal academic tone while preserving the mentions of NVivo 11 and the coding of 121 articles verbatim.
can you rewrite this paragraph in a formal academic tone only? i don't need novice or intermediate versions—just one polished version.
Sure. Share the paragraph and I’ll provide a single formal academic rewrite.
yeah, earlier i asked for novice/intermediate just because my supervisor wanted examples. personally i prefer one strong academic rewrite instead of multiple levels.
Got it—going forward I’ll default to one polished academic version unless you explicitly ask for different proficiency levels.
